clinical_history: 77-year old woman with rheumatoid arthritis, chest pain, and dyspnea.  What is the most likely diagnosis?
diagnosis: c)  There are diffuse ST elevations (I, II, aVL, aVF, V2-V6) with associated PR segment deviations (elevated PR in aVR; depressed in V4-V6).  Borderline low voltage and sinus tachycardia raise question of pericardial effusion, rule out cardiac tamponade.  On echocardiogram, a small to moderate effusion was present without hemodynamic compromise.  (Note: Left atrial abnormality is also present -- the patient had underlying aortic stenosis and mitral regurgitation.)
